intro: Five dairy- and egg-free dinners built around this week's Publix BOGOs and Kroger deals.

recipes.title: BBQ Chicken with Charred Corn & Crispy Potatoes
recipes.blurb: Grilled (or seared) chicken glazed with BBQ sauce, with charred sweet corn and smashed roasted potatoes.
steps: Boil potatoes whole until just fork-tender, ~15 min. Drain, smash lightly, toss with olive oil and seasoning, and roast at 450°F until crispy, ~20 min.
Season chicken with Fire & Smoke rub. Grill or sear over medium-high heat until cooked through (165°F), brushing with BBQ sauce in the last few minutes.
Char the corn alongside the chicken, turning until spotted all over. Rest the chicken 5 min, glaze once more, and serve.
recipes.note: Sweet Baby Ray's original is dairy/egg-free; skip any butter on the corn — olive oil + seasoning works great.

recipes.title: Shrimp Tacos with Avocado-Lime Crema
recipes.blurb: Crispy or sautéed shrimp in crunchy taco shells with a dairy-free avocado crema standing in for sour cream.

saleIngredients.item: Publix Medium Shrimp Platter (or Gorton's Shrimp)
Old El Paso Taco Shells or Tortillas

steps: Blend one avocado with the juice of a lime, a handful of cilantro, and a pinch of salt until smooth — that's the crema.
Toss shrimp with chili powder and cumin, then sauté 2–3 min per side (or bake breaded shrimp per the box).
Warm the shells, fill with cabbage, shrimp, and crema. Serve with watermelon on the side.
recipes.note: Plain shrimp is safe; if you go with Gorton's breaded shrimp, check the label — some breaded varieties contain dairy.

recipes.title: Smoky Kielbasa & Tomato Rice Skillet
recipes.blurb: One-pan browned kielbasa simmered with tomatoes, peppers, and rice.

saleIngredients.item: Hillshire Farm Smoked Sausage or Polska Kielbasa

steps: Slice kielbasa into coins and brown in olive oil; set aside.
Sauté onion, pepper, and garlic in the same pan. Stir in 1 cup rice and a teaspoon of smoked paprika.
Add the can of tomatoes plus 1½ cups broth, return the kielbasa, cover, and simmer ~20 min until the rice is tender.
recipes.note: Stick with the original smoked sausage — skip the cheddar variety.

recipes.title: Backyard Burger Night
recipes.blurb: Classic grilled burgers with grillin' beans and corn — condiments are on sale too.

saleIngredients.item: Bush's Best Baked or Grillin' Beans

steps: Form ¼-lb patties, season generously, and press a dimple into the center of each.
Grill 3–4 min per side. Warm the beans in a pot on the side of the grill and char the corn.
Toast the buns, build with lettuce, tomato, onion, pickles — skip cheese and mayo.
recipes.note: Nature's Own classic buns are typically dairy/egg-free, but check the label on the variety you grab. Veggie swap: Actual Veggies Black Bean Burgers are also BOGO at Publix this week.

recipes.title: Sticky BBQ Ribs with Corn & Watermelon
recipes.blurb: Low-effort oven ribs (they come pre-seasoned in the bag) finished with a sticky glaze.

saleIngredients.item: Publix St. Louis Style Pork Spareribs (frozen, in-bag)
Kosmos Q Rub or BBQ Sauce

steps: Thaw ribs overnight in the fridge. Add extra rub if you like, wrap tightly in foil, and bake at 300°F for ~2½ hours until tender.
Unwrap, brush with BBQ sauce, and broil (or grill) 5 min until sticky and caramelized.
Serve with charred corn and cold watermelon.
recipes.note: The Prairie Fresh pre-seasoned ribs (also BOGO) work identically — glance at the marinade ingredients to confirm no dairy.
